summary_one_line: Input must contain a PNG chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the iCCP FOURCC so chunk_name == png_iCCP holds.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG chunk-header whose 32-bit type field byte-equals the ASCII FOURCC 'iCCP', so the dispatcher's else-if equality test chunk_name == png_iCCP resolves true and png_handle_iCCP runs.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves the branch in 9/10 trials and is the only winner; the dispatch comparison chunk_name == png_iCCP is an exact 4-byte integer equality that I2SRandReplace can satisfy by substituting the logged png_iCCP operand into the chunk-type bytes of an existing valid PNG seed (header magic 89 50 4e 47 present in every winning seed).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the true side in all 10 trials (resolved=0/10), reaching only the false arm: in DIVERGENT BRANCHES line 897 shows L T=0 F=80, i.e. naive's havoc mutation never lands the exact iCCP type-word so the equality test stays false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer CMP at the dispatch and records both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the matched operand and substitutes the other (the iCCP literal) at the chunk-type offset, hitting the magic-number equality that naive's blind byte mutation cannot brute-force.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is an exact equality test against the iCCP chunk constant.
cmplog is the decisive winner over naive on this branch via the I2S technique.
cmplog resolves the branch in 9 of 10 trials while naive never does.
naive blocks the true side in every trial, never matching the iCCP type word.
At line 897 the winner takes the true side 10 times while the loser takes it zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 897  T=10 F=58  T=0 F=80  else if (chunk_name == png_iCCP)

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry the valid PNG header magic that anchors I2S substitution of the chunk-type bytes.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets, the mechanism that lands the exact iCCP literal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3900 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed db0befcb8f71d9dd` and observed two I2S-floor rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 5 and depth 6 of the ancestor chain, the dash-row signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose dispatch compares chunk_name against the iCCP constant where naive resolves the true branch as often as cmplog within the same budget, showing the exact 4-byte equality is not an I2S-specific roadblock.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section shows W/L differences clustered in the IHDR width/height region (0x13-0x2e) rather than directly exposing the iCCP type-word offset, so the precise substituted-byte location is inferred from the dispatch equality rather than directly visible in BYTE DIFF.
